sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 27 - Actuarial Guideline for Accelerated Benefits.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 27 - Actuarial Guideline for Accelerated Benefits

observedSectionWindows.note: Three accelerated-benefit categories plus the non-discounted and actuarially discounted reserve framing.
Interest accrual approach to financing acceleration of benefits and the rate-method disclosure boundary.
Benefit payment liens, premium and loan interactions, RPU / ETI options, and the closing boundary.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 27 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 28 or later guideline files.
Keep the active guideline posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer is noisy / encoded.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat express duty, allowance, cap, or required disclosure language as regulatory text when the guideline says it must be followed.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat accelerated-benefit categories, lien, RPU, ETI, and related terms as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat the reserve discussion and aggregate-reserve framing as structure until the text clearly states a separate duty.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the benefit reduction, lien handling, and accelerated-payment mechanics as calculation structure until a duty is explicit.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat formula labels, interest accrual, and reserve comparisons as context until interpretation is explicit.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat contract and actuarial memorandum disclosure language as documentation expectation when the guideline says the method should be specified.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to Standard Valuation Law, policy provisions, actuarial memorandums, RPU, and ETI as cross-reference context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat title, framing, and explanatory notes as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page 1-5 window as a review-only slice and do not split it unless a later cleanup pass explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the OCR noise, interest accrual language, lien mechanics, and termination conditions as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 27 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch remains review-only by default.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 27 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag27-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-107 through batch-109 slices when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 27 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch IDs.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 27 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 27 planning artifact. Planned batches 107-109 remain review-only by default and are not an extraction run.
